name: AutoScientist Deep Refractory Rheumatoid Arthritis Case Study
purpose: Evaluate whether AutoScientist can produce an audit-ready, multi-agent, tool-heavy biomedical strategy dossier for refractory rheumatoid arthritis rather than a shallow target summary.

primary_sources.name: Open Targets Platform target-disease evidence
primary_sources.role: Target-disease association, known-drug, genetic, literature, and clinical evidence context.

primary_sources.name: ClinicalTrials.gov API
primary_sources.role: Clinical study metadata for refractory rheumatoid arthritis intervention and comparator context.

primary_sources.name: PubMed / NCBI E-utilities
primary_sources.role: Literature retrieval, target mechanism evidence, safety evidence, and translational caveats.

primary_sources.name: openFDA adverse event API
primary_sources.role: Safety-signal retrieval for candidate interventions and target-class therapies.

primary_sources.name: GRADE evidence certainty framing
primary_sources.role: High/moderate/low/very-low evidence certainty concepts for calibrated claims.

selection_policy.expanded_task_policy: One deep case expanded into four complementary tasks; each task should be run across full and ablation modes.
gold_label_policy: The system should support established TNF biology in rheumatoid arthritis but not claim TNF is sufficient for refractory disease.
The system should compare non-TNF mechanisms and patient stratification needs rather than overfitting to the seed target.
The system should explicitly handle non-response, secondary loss of response, infection/malignancy safety concerns, and trial/real-world evidence limitations.
The system should propose decision-grade experiments and abstain from any patient-treatment recommendation.
rationale: This case is intentionally harder than a single target-disease validation benchmark.
Refractory rheumatoid arthritis requires multi-target reasoning, clinical translation caveats, and patient-subtype thinking.
The run should demonstrate long-horizon tool use, memory, replay, provenance, and calibrated uncertainty.

task_templates.objective_template: Build an audit-ready scientific strategy dossier for rheumatoid arthritis patients with inadequate response or loss of response to TNF inhibitors and JAK inhibitors. Use {gene_symbol} as the established inflammatory anchor but compare alternative mechanisms including IL6/IL6R, JAK/STAT, GM-CSF/CSF2, CD20/B-cell depletion, CTLA4 costimulation, TYK2, IL23/Th17 biology, synovial fibroblast pathways, macrophage polarization, and tissue-resident immune mechanisms. Separate established clinical precedence from novel repurposing hypotheses, identify which patient subtypes each mechanism might fit, retrieve public target-disease evidence, clinical-trial or drug-precedence evidence, safety signals, counterevidence, and propose a ranked research strategy with calibrated confidence.

Create a mechanism map for rheumatoid arthritis treatment non-response and secondary loss of response using {gene_symbol} as the reference pathway. Compare cytokine redundancy, B-cell biology, fibroblast-like synoviocyte activation, macrophage and myeloid pathways, JAK/STAT signaling, interferon signatures, Fc receptor biology, tissue hypoxia/metabolism, and pain/inflammation dissociation. For each mechanism, state public evidence strength, clinical or genetic support, counterevidence, safety liabilities, and what data would falsify the mechanism as a useful next hypothesis.

Identify and triage drug-repurposing or combination-therapy hypotheses for refractory rheumatoid arthritis, anchored on {gene_symbol} biology but explicitly considering non-TNF mechanisms. Retrieve public evidence for candidate interventions or target classes, open safety signals, clinical-trial context, contraindication-style caveats, and translational feasibility. Rank hypotheses by evidence quality, novelty, patient-subtype specificity, safety burden, and falsifiability. Do not recommend patient treatment; produce research hypotheses only.

Design a decision-grade experimental program to test the top refractory rheumatoid arthritis hypotheses from public evidence. The plan must include human-data reanalysis, synovial tissue or single-cell validation, perturbation assays, safety or off-target checks, patient-stratification biomarkers, controls, negative controls, success criteria, failure criteria, and how each experiment would update confidence. Use {gene_symbol} as the reference comparator but require the system to justify any non-TNF hypothesis with evidence and uncertainty.

seed_cases.case_rationale: TNF is an established rheumatoid arthritis target, but refractory disease is not solved by restating TNF validity; a credible system must compare alternative mechanisms, safety constraints, and decision-grade validation paths.

caveats: This benchmark tests research triage and hypothesis generation, not clinical decision support.
The seed target anchors public evidence retrieval, but the objectives require comparison against alternative mechanisms rather than hardcoding target-specific rules.
Scores are credibility and auditability indicators; expert biomedical review remains required before sharing scientific claims.
